compiled_state.title: As a developer, worktree harvesting and story/bug merges record their outcome via WORKTREE_TRACKING:complete and :landed comments
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Modifies harvest-worktree.sh and its caller (per-worktree-review-commit.md) to write WORKTREE_TRACKING:complete on all exit paths, and adds WORKTREE_TRACKING:landed writes to the story/bug merge-to-main flow.

**Why**: Closes the tracking loop — without :complete and :landed comments, the resume scanner cannot distinguish abandoned branches from completed work. Stories A (format spec) and B together form the full comment lifecycle contract.

**Scope**:
- IN: WORKTREE_TRACKING:complete written with outcome=merged|discarded on every harvest-worktree.sh exit (success, failure, SIGTERM); per-worktree-review-commit.md updated to either pass ticket ID to harvest-worktree.sh or write the :complete comment itself after harvest returns, including the failure path (outcome=discarded); WORKTREE_TRACKING:landed written on story/bug tickets in the session-close flow (end-session/SKILL.md or equivalent) after merge-to-main.sh returns success; fail-silent writes when .tickets-tracker/ is unavailable
- OUT: Writing :start comments (Story A); detecting or acting on comment lifecycle (Story C)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, harvest-worktree.sh writes a WORKTREE_TRACKING:complete comment on the task ticket on every exit path (success, failure, SIGTERM), recording the branch name and outcome (merged or discarded) in the format defined by Story A's spec; per-worktree-review-commit.md is updated to supply the ticket ID and to write :complete with outcome=discarded on its failure path
  ← Satisfies: "When work completes via harvest-worktree.sh (on any exit path — success or failure), a WORKTREE_TRACKING:complete comment is written with the branch name and outcome (merged or discarded)."

- When this story is complete, after a story's or bug's changes are merged to main (via the session-close flow), a WORKTREE_TRACKING:landed comment is written on each affected story/bug ticket before session cleanup completes; the session-close flow (end-session/SKILL.md or equivalent) iterates over the set of story/bug tickets completed in the session and writes :landed after merge-to-main.sh returns success
  ← Satisfies: "After a story's or bug's changes are merged to main, a WORKTREE_TRACKING:landed comment is written on that ticket confirming the merge completed."

- When this story is complete, :complete and :landed writes fail silently when .tickets-tracker/ is unavailable
  ← Satisfies: "When ticket comment writes fail (e.g., .tickets-tracker/ unavailable), WORKTREE_TRACKING writes fail silently without blocking sub-agent work."

- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Implementation] Whether :complete logic lives inside harvest-worktree.sh (via a new --ticket-id parameter) or in per-worktree-review-commit.md after harvest returns is an implementation decision for /dso:implementation-plan; either way per-worktree-review-commit.md must be in scope and its failure path must write outcome=discarded
- [Reliability] harvest-worktree.sh already has an ERR/EXIT trap; any :complete write via that trap must degrade gracefully if the ticket ID variable is not yet set (very early exit before ticket ID is known)
- [Implementation] The :landed write occurs in the session-close flow, not in merge-to-main.sh itself (which is ticket-ID-unaware); the host is end-session/SKILL.md Step 4 post-merge or an equivalent session-cleanup step

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
